steps.title: Identify the inequality from the boundary line
steps.content:
The boundary line passes through (0, -4) and (2, 4).

Slope: $$\frac{4-(-4)}{2-0} = 4$$
Equation: $$y = 4x - 4$$
The shaded region lies below the line, so solutions must satisfy $y < 4x - 4$.

steps.title: Test answer choices
steps.content:
For each choice, check if $y < 4x - 4$:
  • A $(-5, -6)$: $-6 < 4(-5) - 4 = -24$? No.
  • B $(-2, 5)$: $5 < 4(-2) - 4 = -12$? No.
  • C $(1, 4)$: $4 < 4(1) - 4 = 0$? No.
  • D $(6, -2)$: $-2 < 4(6) - 4 = 20$? Yes.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
